title: TC-DFU-UI-001: Post-DFU Button State — Charge Case Update Success Disables Update Button
steps: Precondition: Device (Voyager Legend 50) is connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Charge Case firmware update is available.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update
Tap 'Update' to start Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for update to complete successfully (100%, device restarts).
Observe the Firmware Update screen after device reconnects.
Do NOT navigate away or restart the app.
Verify the 'Update' button state.
expected: Expected: After successful Charge Case firmware update, the 'Update' button is either disabled (greyed out) or removed entirely, and the screen shows 'Firmware is up to date' or equivalent. Actual (If Bug): The 'Update' button remains tappable and appears active, showing stale pre-update state.

title: TC-DFU-UI-002: Re-Tap After Successful Update — Immediate Failure Without App Restart
steps: Precondition: Device (Voyager Legend 50) connected. Charge Case firmware update just completed successfully (from TC-DFU-UI-001).
Without restarting the app or navigating away, tap the 'Update' button again.
Observe the app response.
expected: Expected: The 'Update' button is disabled/non-functional, or tapping it shows a toast/message 'Firmware is already up to date'. Actual (If Bug): Tapping the button triggers an immediate failure — error dialog, crash, or stuck state — and the update cannot proceed until the app is force-killed and relaunched.

title: TC-DFU-UI-003: App Restart Recovery — Stale Button State Cleared After Kill/Relaunch
steps: Precondition: Device (Voyager Legend 50) connected. Charge Case firmware update completed successfully. The 'Update' button is still visible and tappable (stale state from bug).
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recents).
Relaunch the app and reconnect to Voyager Legend 50.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update
Observe the 'Update' button state.
expected: Expected: After app restart, the 'Update' button is properly disabled/removed, and the screen reflects the current firmware version. The stale state is cleared. Actual (If Bug): The button remains active even after restart, or the app shows inconsistent firmware version info.

title: TC-DFU-UI-004: Firmware Version Cache Validation — App vs Device Reconciliation Post-DFU
steps: Precondition: Device (Voyager Legend 50) connected. Note current Charge Case firmware version displayed in app (e.g., v1.0.0).
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update
Perform a successful Charge Case firmware update.
After update completes and device reconnects, navigate to: Device → About / Device Info
Read the displayed Charge Case firmware version.
Compare with the pre-update version.
expected: Expected: The app displays the NEW firmware version (e.g., v1.1.0) matching the device's actual firmware. The app's internal firmware version cache is refreshed post-update. Actual (If Bug): The app still shows the OLD firmware version (v1.0.0), indicating the version cache was not updated after DFU completion, which correlates with the stale update button state.

title: TC-DFU-UI-005: Post-DFU Button State Across App Lifecycle — Background/Foreground
steps: Precondition: Device (Voyager Legend 50) connected. Charge Case firmware update completed successfully.
Without force-killing, press Home button to background the app.
Wait 30 seconds.
Reopen the app from recent apps (foreground).
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update
Observe the 'Update' button state.
expected: Expected: After backgrounding and foregrounding, the 'Update' button remains disabled/removed. The app correctly retains the post-DFU state across lifecycle transitions. Actual (If Bug): The button re-appears as active after foregrounding, indicating the app re-fetches stale cached state on resume.

title: TC-DFU-UI-006: Post-DFU Button State Across Bluetooth Disconnect/Reconnect
steps: Precondition: Device (Voyager Legend 50) connected. Charge Case firmware update completed successfully.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update — verify button is disabled/removed.
Disconnect Bluetooth from phone settings (or turn off device).
Wait 10 seconds.
Reconnect Bluetooth (or turn on device).
Wait for app to reconnect automatically.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update
Observe the 'Update' button state.
expected: Expected: After Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect, the 'Update' button remains disabled/removed. The app correctly re-syncs state with the device on reconnection. Actual (If Bug): The button re-appears as active after reconnection, indicating the app does not re-validate firmware version on Bluetooth re-sync.

title: TC-DFU-UI-007: Immediate State Trigger — Kill App Mid-Update Completion, Verify Button on Relaunch
steps: Precondition: Device (Voyager Legend 50) connected. Charge Case firmware update is in progress (e.g., at 60%).
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recents) while the update is still running.
Wait for the device to complete the update independently (device will finish and restart).
Relaunch the app and reconnect to Voyager Legend 50.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update
Observe the 'Update' button state and displayed firmware version.
expected: Expected: The app detects the device's current firmware version on reconnection and shows 'Firmware is up to date' with the button disabled/removed. The app does not show a stale 'update available' state. Actual (If Bug): The app shows the update as still available (button active) because it lost the in-progress state during kill and never received the completion callback.

title: TC-DFU-UI-008: Post-DFU Button State After Phone Reboot
steps: Precondition: Device (Voyager Legend 50) connected. Charge Case firmware update completed successfully.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update — verify button is disabled/removed.
Reboot the phone.
After phone restarts, launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Connect to Voyager Legend 50.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update
Observe the 'Update' button state and displayed firmware version.
expected: Expected: After phone reboot, the app correctly reads the device's current firmware version and shows 'Firmware is up to date' with the button disabled/removed. Actual (If Bug): The app shows stale pre-update state with the button active, indicating the app relies on in-memory state that is lost on reboot and not re-fetched from the device.
